verdict: Pure-Python BPE trained on 1 book only (full corpus infeasible); worst fertility/utilization of the 5 candidates; validates the from-scratch implementation, not a real candidate.
Fertility 1.612 tok/word on held-out books, 87.7% vocab utilization; smallest embedding table but noticeably worse fertility/rare-word splitting than 16k; not chosen.
Fertility 1.500 tok/word on held-out books, 71.3% vocab utilization, 3.80 avg pieces/rare-word; best balance of fertility/rare-word gains vs embedding-table cost; CHOSEN as project tokenizer (D-014).
Fertility 1.427 tok/word on held-out books but only 49.3% vocab utilization and up to 49% of a 100M param budget in the embedding table; diminishing returns over 16k; not chosen.
OpenAI's tokenizer, never trained on our corpus; only 30.6% vocab utilization on our held-out text despite 50k vocab, confirming domain mismatch; reference only, not a candidate.
